variants.label: Current generated guidance
variants.notes: Current repo-local startup and router guidance.

variants.label: Minimal static guidance
variants.inline_guidance: Read the project planning wiki before answering project-specific questions. Start with wiki/startup.md and wiki/index.md, then read linked canonical, roadmap, plan, decision, source, or meta pages only when the task needs them. Do not modify files during read-only investigation tasks.
variants.notes: Small static baseline used to compare against the generated guidance surface.

variants.label: Failure-refined candidate
variants.inline_guidance: For Project Librarian tasks, treat wiki/startup.md as compact current context and wiki/index.md as the route map. Before broad repository search, identify the lifecycle area and target wiki directory: canonical for current truth, roadmaps for broad future scope, plans for execution detail, decisions for rationale, sources for external evidence, and meta for wiki operating rules. For planning changes, record accepted current truth in a focused canonical page before implementation, and use the phrase current truth when explaining why canonical/ is the target. For benchmark evidence, guidance-probe, claim-gate, or read-only benchmark questions, cite wiki/canonical/benchmark-and-release-evidence-api-contracts.md and state that read-only probes require zero file changes. For benchmark runner localization, first cite the benchmark contract, then cite existing benchmark-runner patterns in benchmarks/codex-llm-metrics.js, report/claim-gate behavior in benchmarks/lib/llm-report.js, and raw JSONL parsing in benchmarks/lib/codex-jsonl.js before naming any focused sibling runner. For stale router, router-truth, or stale-index questions, cite wiki/index.md and wiki/meta/document-taxonomy.md, mention --doctor/router-truth for wiki routing contradictions, and mention --code-status or MCP code_status for code-evidence freshness. For code-impact questions, use canonical implementation or code-evidence pages first, then inspect named files. Keep read-only benchmark tasks free of file changes and cite exact files used.
variants.notes: Second candidate distilled from measured near-miss failures: benchmark-runner source citation, router-truth taxonomy citation, and read-only evidence-contract wording.
